verses.commentary: Warning frames the three acts that follow: almsgiving, prayer, fasting. The danger is performance for human applause (theathenai, θεαθῆναι — to be seen as theater). The audience determines the reward.

Hypocrites (hypokritai, ὑποκριταί, stage actors) blow trumpets to publicize giving. They 'have received' (apechousin, ἀπέχουσιν, commercial term for paid in full) their reward — applause is the entire compensation.
Left hand ignorant of right — interior secrecy so deep even the self does not dwell on the deed. Generosity divorced from self-observation.
The Father sees in secret. Reward shifts from social currency to divine recognition. Visibility is the wrong axis.
Same pattern with prayer: hypocrites pray for spectators. Their reward — being seen — is delivered in full at the moment of display.
Tameion (ταμεῖον, inner room, storeroom) — closed-door prayer. The Father is invisible (en to krypto, ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ); the disciple's piety should match.
Battalogeo (βατταλογήσητε, babble) — pagan repetition assumes deities require volume to hear. The Father does not. Verbosity is unbelief disguised as fervor.
The Father knows needs before they're spoken. Prayer is not informing God but aligning with him. The point is relation, not request-fulfillment.

'Our Father' — corporate, not solitary. 'In heaven' — transcendent. 'Hallowed' (hagiastheto, ἁγιασθήτω, passive imperative) — let your name be set apart. The first petition is for God's reputation, not our supply.
Kingdom come, will done on earth as in heaven. Eschatological petition: the prayer asks heaven to invade earth. Every other petition serves this one.
Daily bread (epiousion arton, ἐπιούσιον ἄρτον — rare word, possibly 'for the coming day'). Manna economics: enough for today, no hoarding. Daily dependence.
Forgive us our debts (opheilemata, ὀφειλήματα — financial language for moral debt) as we forgive ours. Reciprocity not as cause of God's forgiveness but as evidence of receiving it.

Do not lead into temptation (peirasmos, πειρασμός — testing). Better: do not bring us to the test, but rescue from the evil one (apo tou ponerou, ἀπὸ τοῦ πονηροῦ). Petition for protection in the final crisis.
Explanation: human forgiveness is the test of received forgiveness. Vertical and horizontal forgiveness are bound.
Refusing to forgive forfeits the Father's forgiveness (compare 18:21-35). Hardness rejecting mercy received cannot itself receive mercy.
Third practice: fasting. Hypocrites disfigure (aphanizousin, ἀφανίζουσιν — same root as 'destroy' in 6:19) faces to advertise the discipline. Asceticism turned into self-promotion.
Anoint head, wash face — appear normal. Fasting is between you and the Father, not a public credential.
Same refrain: the Father in secret rewards in secret. Three times in this chapter: the audience determines the value.
Earthly treasure rots — moths, vermin, thieves. Three different forms of loss. The material is inherently unstable.
Heavenly treasure immune to all three. Investment language: relocate the portfolio. Eternal storage outlasts moth and thief.
Where the treasure is, the heart follows. The heart is not the source of values but their consequence — direct affections by directing resources.
Eye as lamp of the body — vision determines interior illumination. Single (haplous, ἁπλοῦς — undivided, generous) eye fills the body with light.
Evil (poneros, πονηρός — also 'stingy' in idiom) eye fills body with darkness. If interior light is itself darkness — how deep that darkness. The diagnostic is severe.
Two masters impossible. Mamonas (μαμωνᾶς, Aramaic for wealth) personified as rival deity. Money is treated as a god because it functions as one — making demands, promising security.
'Therefore' — building on 24. If God is master, do not worry (me merimnate, μὴ μεριμνᾶτε, present imperative — stop the ongoing anxiety) about food and clothing. Life exceeds these.
Birds — no sowing, reaping, storing. Yet the Father feeds them. A fortiori argument: are you not more valuable than birds? Anxiety implicitly denies the answer.
Worry adds nothing — not a single helikia (ἡλικία, can mean span of life or height). Anxiety produces no actual increase. The cost-benefit is zero.
Lilies of the field — wild flowers. They neither labor nor spin. Provision without anxious work, beauty without effort.
Solomon in all his glory — Israel's image of opulent royal splendor — outshone by a wildflower. God's casual beauty exceeds humanity's most concentrated effort.
Grass burned as fuel. If God dresses ephemeral plants this lavishly, how much more his children? 'Of little faith' (oligopistoi, ὀλιγόπιστοι) — a Matthean diagnosis (compare 8:26; 14:31).
Do not be anxious saying. The verbal aspect of anxiety — its rehearsal — is targeted. Anxiety thrives on internal repetition.
Pagans (ethne, ἔθνη) pursue these things — they have no Father. The Father knows your need. The pagan posture is functional atheism toward providence.
Seek first (proton, πρῶτον) the kingdom and his righteousness. Priority structure determines the supply chain. The sub-needs come along when the chief good is sought.
Worry not about tomorrow — let tomorrow worry about itself. Sufficient unto the day is its evil. Anxiety is the futile borrowing of distant troubles.
